title: Bug fix with tests: off-by-one + timezone bug in date-range util
strength: Root-cause a failing test in seeded buggy code without breaking the green ones.
prompt: This project has a date utility in src/daterange.js with two bugs. `daysBetween(a, b)` is meant to return the number of whole calendar days from date `a` to date `b` (inclusive of neither endpoint difference rounding — a pure day delta), but it is off by one and also mishandles dates that straddle a daylight-saving boundary because it divides millisecond differences. `eachDay(a, b)` should yield every calendar date from `a` to `b` inclusive as YYYY-MM-DD strings, but it currently skips the final day and can loop forever across DST. There are failing tests in test/daterange.test.js that pin the correct behaviour. Do NOT edit the tests. Find the root cause of each bug, fix src/daterange.js so all tests pass, and keep the previously-passing tests green. Use UTC-based date math to be DST-safe.
verify: git diff --quiet -- test/daterange.test.js || (echo 'tests were modified - not allowed' && false)
